name: Cinnamon Toast Crunch
notes: Per label: 1 cup (40g) = 170 cal, 4g fat, 33g carbs, 2g fiber, 12g sugar, 2g protein. 1.5 cups = 255 cal, 6g fat, 49.5g carbs, 3g fiber, 18g sugar, 3g protein. 3/4 cup 2% milk = 92 cal, 3.6g fat, 10g carbs, 3.6g sugar, 6g protein. Combined = 347 cal. DB value of 345 is essentially correct.

name: Cocoa Puffs
notes: Per label: 1 cup (36g) = 140 cal, 2g fat, 31g carbs, 2g fiber, 12g sugar, 2g protein. 1.5 cups = 210 cal, 3g fat, 46.5g carbs, 3g fiber, 18g sugar, 3g protein. 3/4 cup 2% milk = 92 cal, 3.6g fat, 10g carbs, 3.6g sugar, 6g protein. Combined = 302 cal. DB value of 300 is essentially correct.

name: Frosted Flakes
notes: Per label: 1 cup (39g) = 140 cal, 0g fat, 34g carbs, 0g fiber, 14g sugar, 2g protein. 1.5 cups = 210 cal, 0g fat, 51g carbs, 0g fiber, 21g sugar, 3g protein. 3/4 cup 2% milk = 92 cal, 3.6g fat, 10g carbs, 3.6g sugar, 6g protein. Combined = 302 cal. DB value of 315 is ~13 cal too high.

name: Lucky Charms
notes: Per label: 1 cup (36g) = 140 cal, 1.5g fat, 30g carbs, 2g fiber, 12g sugar, 3g protein. 1.5 cups = 210 cal, 2.25g fat, 45g carbs, 3g fiber, 18g sugar, 4.5g protein. 3/4 cup 2% milk = 92 cal, 3.6g fat, 10g carbs, 3.6g sugar, 6g protein. Combined = 302 cal. DB value of 300 is essentially correct.

name: Rold Gold Tiny Twists Pretzels (single serve)
notes: Standard single-serve bags come in 1 oz and 2 oz sizes. No 1.75 oz bag exists. DB entry of 1.75 oz / 193 cal does not match a real retail package. If someone eats a 2 oz bag: 220 cal. The DB 1.75 oz entry at 193 cal is proportionally correct (110 cal/oz * 1.75 = 193), but the serving size is not a standard retail package.

name: Rold Gold Tiny Twists Pretzels (1 oz)
notes: DB says 120 cal for 1 oz but actual is 110 cal per 1 oz serving. DB is 10 cal too high.

name: Blue Diamond Roasted Almonds
notes: Per label: 1 oz (28g, ~28 nuts) = 170 cal, 6g protein, 6g carbs (3g fiber, 1g sugar), 14g fat. 2 oz = 340 cal. Oven roasted variety is 160 cal/oz. DB value of 330 is close but slightly low for roasted salted (340) or correct for oven roasted plain (320). Using roasted salted as more common retail variety.

name: Peanut Butter (365 Whole Foods)
notes: 365 Organic Creamy Peanut Butter (Unsweetened) = 200 cal per 2 tbsp (32g). The unsweetened & no salt version is 190 cal. DB value of 200 cal matches the unsweetened (salted) version exactly.

name: Oscar Mayer Classic Wieners
notes: Multiple sources show 110 cal per link, not 120. The Classic Wieners (turkey/chicken/pork blend) are 110 cal. Oscar Mayer Beef Franks are 148 cal per link (45g). DB value of 120 is 10 cal too high for Classic Wieners.

name: Guacamole + Chips (Wholly Guacamole)
verified_serving: 1/2 of 8oz tub (4 servings guac = 8 tbsp/120g) + ~28 tortilla chips (2oz)
notes: Wholly Guacamole Classic: 70 cal per 2 tbsp (30g). 8 oz tub = 8 servings. Half tub = 4 servings = 280 cal guac (24g fat, 8g carbs, 4g protein, 4g fiber). For chips to eat half a tub: ~2 oz tortilla chips = ~280 cal (14g fat, 40g carbs, 2g protein, 2g fiber). Total = ~560 cal. DB value of 440 significantly underestimates the chip portion. If using fewer chips (~1 oz), total would be ~420 cal which is closer to DB.

name: Mission Super Soft Large Flour Tortilla
notes: Mission Super Soft Flour Tortillas Burrito Size = 200 cal per tortilla. DB value of 210 is 10 cal high. The soft taco size (smaller, 8 inch) is 140 cal. DB likely intended the large burrito size.

name: Tortilla Chips + Queso (restaurant to-go)
verified_serving: ~3 oz chips (1 basket, ~42 chips) + ~4 oz queso dip
notes: A restaurant basket of tortilla chips is typically 3-4 oz (~420-560 cal). Chili's lists their basket at 400 cal for chips alone. Queso dip: Applebee's queso = 334 cal per serving. A realistic restaurant portion of chips + queso is 600-900 cal depending on the restaurant. DB value of 430 is significantly underestimated. However, this is inherently variable. If to-go means a small container (2 oz chips + 2 oz queso), ~430 cal could be plausible. Marking as needs_update since most restaurant portions are much larger.

name: Chocolate Bar + Milk (Convenience Combo)
verified_serving: 1 standard Hershey's Milk Chocolate bar (1.55 oz/44g) + 12 oz 2% milk
notes: Hershey's standard bar (1.55 oz) = 220 cal + 12 oz 2% milk = 183 cal = 403 total. If using Snickers (1.86 oz, 250 cal) + 12 oz 2% milk (183 cal) = 433 cal. DB value of 390 is slightly low for either combo. This does appear to be a synthetic combo item. With Hershey's it is 403 cal (close to DB). With a Snickers it is 433 cal. DB is approximately correct for a Hershey's bar combo.
